attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who selectively targets KOM points through calculated mid-race efforts, often breaking from established breaks to contest intermediate sprints. Shows willingness to spend energy chasing classification jerseys rather than stage wins.
team_tactics: Deployed in dual roles: early-race KOM hunting in stage races and domestique work in sprint train positioning for team leaders. Team priorities override personal classification ambitions when conflicts arise, as seen when waiting for protected climbers.
terrain_preference: Excels in rolling terrain requiring repeated bridging efforts and tactical positioning for intermediate points. Emerging capability on selective strato/hard one-day classics. Less effective on pure summit finishes where extended climbing follows KOM efforts.
vulnerability: Energy management when chasing KOM points leaves him vulnerable in final kilometers, repeatedly caught within 3km after burning matches on intermediate sprints. Personal ambitions subordinated to team needs at critical moments.
key_patterns: Breaks from established breakaways to contest KOM points rather than stay cooperative (3 observations)
Demonstrates exceptional recovery ability, pacing back from significant time deficits to contest intermediate targets
Secured mountains classification through aggressive early positioning, wrapping up jersey before final stages
Transitions between leadership roles (KOM hunter) and domestique duties (sprint train) across different races
Shows sprint capacity on flatter finishes when fresh, able to contest bunch kicks
form_trajectory: neutral: transitioned from classification contender (2022 Tour de Suisse KOM winner) to domestique role supporting sprint train in 2023 Tour, suggesting team tactical repositioning rather than form decline
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 19
